title: What is SQL?
description: The special language used by databases.

slides.title: Speaking Database 🗣️
slides.content:
# The Universal Database Language

How do you ask a database a question? Or tell it to save a new user?

You must speak its language! 

That language is called **SQL** (pronounced "Sequel" or "S-Q-L"). It stands for **Structured Query Language**.

quizData.question: What does SQL stand for?
quizData.explanation: SQL stands for Structured Query Language. It's the standard language for asking relational databases questions (queries).

slides.title: Simple Commands 📜
slides.content:
## Readable Code

The beauty of SQL is that it reads almost like plain English.

Want to see all the users? You literally tell the database: `SELECT * FROM users;`

slides.title: Why Learn SQL? 💼
slides.content: SQL is used practically everywhere! From giant tech companies to small startups. Once you learn SQL, your skills apply to almost every major database (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite) because they all understand SQL!

quizData.question: If you learn SQL for one database system, does it apply to others?

options.text: No, every database has a completely different language
Yes, SQL is practically a universal standard across most relational databases

quizData.explanation: SQL is incredibly versatile. Whether you use MySQL, Postgres, SQL Server, or SQLite, the core SQL commands are practically identical.
